entities.name: hiring bias
entities.context: Systemic bias in recruitment that V argues stems from adversarial dynamics, not just algorithm design

entities.name: adversarial recruitment
entities.context: The gatekeeper-applicant struggle that triggers defensive signaling and reliance on biased proxies

entities.name: partner-explorer collaboration
entities.context: V's proposed alternative model where recruiter-candidate dynamic shifts from adversarial to collaborative

entities.name: coaching-led interaction
entities.context: Non-adversarial approach that increases psychological safety and surfaces authentic candidate capabilities

entities.name: psychological safety
entities.context: Key enabler that allows candidates to present true capabilities rather than curated masks

entities.name: proxy-based evaluation
entities.context: Current 'checking boxes' approach that relies on prestige brand names and other biased proxies

entities.name: insight-based evaluation
entities.context: Proposed 'understanding potential' approach that bypasses traditional bias mechanisms

entities.name: defensive signaling
entities.context: Behavior triggered by adversarial systems where candidates use 'safe' proxies to mitigate risk

entities.name: prestige proxies
entities.context: Brand names and credentials that serve as primary carriers of systemic bias in hiring

entities.name: blind algorithms
entities.context: Conventional approach to bias reduction that V argues is less effective than systemic change

entities.name: DE&I compliance algorithms
entities.context: Complex tools that may become obsolete if adversarial gap is solved

relationships.context: Adversarial dynamics are the root cause of bias, not algorithm design
Adversarial systems trigger defensive behavior in candidates
Defensive signaling leads to reliance on 'safe' proxies like brand names
Prestige proxies are the primary carriers of systemic bias
Collaborative model is implemented through coaching-led interactions
Safety allows authentic presentation which enables insight-based assessment
Insight-based approach replaces and bypasses proxy-based evaluation
Dismissed as less effective than addressing adversarial dynamics
Solving adversarial gap may render complex compliance tools obsolete
